stories.title: Auto-Update Mechanism
stories.description:
Add a Sparkle-style auto-update system to ToDoozy using electron-updater with GitHub Releases as the update provider.

## Overview
The app periodically checks for updates (every 4 hours while running) and on manual trigger from Settings. When an update is available, a notification dialog shows the version number and release notes. The user can choose to install now (quit & install) or dismiss (reminded on next launch or manual check). A download progress bar shows during the update download.

## Update Provider
- Use electron-updater with GitHub Releases as the provider
- Configure publish settings in electron-builder config for GitHub
- Ad-hoc code signing is sufficient — electron-updater bypasses Gatekeeper on updates since no quarantine flag is set

## Update Check Triggers
1. On app launch (background check, non-blocking)
2. Every 4 hours while the app is running (setInterval in main process)
3. Manual trigger via 'Check for Updates' button in Settings

## Update Available Dialog
- Modal dialog when update is found
- Shows: current version, new version number, release notes
- Release notes fetched from Supabase (migrate existing What's New content to Supabase table)
- Two buttons: 'Install & Restart' and 'Not Now'
- 'Install & Restart': starts download, shows progress bar, then calls autoUpdater.quitAndInstall()
- 'Not Now': dismisses dialog. User is reminded on next app launch or next manual check (not on the 4-hour periodic check after dismissal — only on launch or manual)

## Download Progress
- Progress bar in the update dialog during download
- Show percentage and download speed if available
- On download error: show error message with 'Retry' button

## Settings Integration
- Add 'Updates' section in Settings
- Display current app version (from app.getVersion())
- 'Check for Updates' button
- States: 'Check for Updates' (idle), 'Checking...' (spinner), 'You're up to date!' (success), or transitions to update dialog

## Release Notes Migration to Supabase
- Create a Supabase table (or use existing mechanism) to store release notes per version
- The update dialog fetches release notes from Supabase for the available version
- Fall back to GitHub Release body if Supabase fetch fails
- The existing in-app What's New tab continues to work but reads from Supabase

## Main Process Implementation
- Configure autoUpdater in main process (src/main/index.ts or dedicated service)
- Handle events: checking-for-update, update-available, update-not-available, download-progress, update-downloaded, error
- IPC channels for renderer communication: check-for-update, update-status, start-download, quit-and-install
- Debounce/gate so multiple check triggers don't stack

## Renderer Implementation
- Update dialog component in src/renderer/src/features/settings/ or shared components
- UpdateStore (Zustand) to manage update state: idle, checking, available, downloading, downloaded, error
- Settings section component for manual check + version display

## Platform
- macOS only for now
- electron-builder publish config targets GitHub Releases

## Files to Create
- src/main/services/UpdateService.ts — wraps autoUpdater, handles all events, IPC communication
- src/renderer/src/features/settings/UpdateSettings.tsx — Settings section with version + check button
- src/renderer/src/shared/components/UpdateDialog.tsx — modal for update available/downloading/error states
- src/renderer/src/shared/stores/updateStore.ts — Zustand store for update state

## Files to Modify
- src/main/index.ts — initialize UpdateService
- src/main/ipc-handlers.ts — add update IPC handlers
- src/preload/index.ts and src/preload/index.d.ts — add update bridge methods
- src/renderer/src/features/settings/UnifiedSettingsModal.tsx — add Updates section
- electron-builder config — add publish config for GitHub Releases
- package.json — add electron-updater dependency

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: electron-updater is installed and configured with GitHub Releases as the provider
App checks for updates on launch and every 4 hours while running
Update-available dialog shows version number, release notes (from Supabase), and 'Install & Restart' / 'Not Now' buttons
'Install & Restart' shows a download progress bar, then quits and installs the update
'Not Now' dismisses the dialog; user is reminded on next launch or manual check
Settings page has a 'Check for Updates' button that triggers a manual check
Settings page displays the current app version
'You're up to date' message shown when no update is available on manual check
Release notes are stored in and fetched from Supabase (migrated from local SQLite)
All update events (checking, downloading, error, up-to-date) are handled with appropriate UI feedback
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
